articles.title: Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs scrubs Instagram account
articles.description:
As of Tuesday afternoon, Diggs's account had zero posts, but still included content on his Instagram Story.

The post Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs scrubs Instagram account appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Stefon Diggs, Patriots wide receiver, deleted all posts from his Instagram account as of Tuesday.
His account still features content on Instagram Story despite the deletion of posts.
This move raises speculation among Boston fans about his future with the Patriots.

articles.title: Get one-way flights for as low as $79 during JetBlue’s spring sale
articles.description:
The sale ends March 5.

The post Get one-way flights for as low as $79 during JetBlue’s spring sale appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: JetBlue offers one-way flights from Boston starting at $79.
The spring sale runs until March 5, 2026.
Boston residents can save on travel for upcoming spring vacations.

articles.title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in March
articles.description:
A full list of what to watch on Netflix, Hulu, Prime Video, HBO Max, Disney+, Paramount+, Apple TV, and Peacock in March 2026.

summary: Discover new streaming options for Boston residents on platforms like Netflix and Hulu in March 2026.
Watch anticipated releases including 10 new movies and 5 original series debuting this month.
Stay updated on local viewing trends as Boston audiences embrace diverse streaming content.

articles.title: Husband and wife charged with throwing rocks at passing cars in Southwick
articles.description:
The Rhode Island couple now faces a slew of felony charges.

The post Husband and wife charged with throwing rocks at passing cars in Southwick appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Husband and wife charged with felony for throwing rocks at cars in Southwick.
Couple faces multiple felony charges, impacting local road safety.
Incident raises concerns among Southwick residents about public safety.

articles.title: With deadline looming, still no word on security funding for World Cup in Foxborough
articles.description:
“We may get a little more meals tax or hotel tax, but this is not a moneymaker for this town. In fact, it's probably more of a headache than it's worth.”

The post With deadline looming, still no word on security funding for World Cup in Foxborough appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Foxborough officials express concerns over World Cup security funding ahead of the event.
Potential increase in meals and hotel tax revenue may not offset local costs.
Residents worry the World Cup could become a financial burden rather than a benefit.
